title: Maximum Level Sum of a Binary Tree
description:
Given the root of a binary tree, the level of its root is 1, the level of its children is 2, and so on.

Return the smallest level x such that the sum of all the values of nodes at level x is maximal.

solutionCode.python:
```python
class TreeNode:
    def __init__(self, x):
        self.val = x
        self.left = None
        self.right = None

def maxLevelSum(root):
    if not root: return 0

    q = [root]
    level, max_sum, result_level = 0, float('-inf'), 0
    
    while q:
        qlen, sum = len(q), 0
        level += 1

        for i in range(qlen):
            temp = q.pop(0)
            sum += temp.val

            if temp.left: q.append(temp.left)
            if temp.right: q.append(temp.right)

        if sum > max_sum:
            max_sum = sum
            result_level = level
    
    return result_level
```
    
    The algorithm uses a queue to perform a level-order traversal (BFS) of the binary tree. At each level, it calculates the sum of the values of nodes and stores the maximum sum found so far, along with the level at which the max sum occurs. 

1. Initialize the queue with the root node.
2. Initialize variables: level = 0, max_sum = minimum possible value (-inf for Python and JavaScript or INT_MIN for C++ and Java), and result_level = 0.
3. Perform a level-order traversal (BFS) of the binary tree. For each level:
   a. Calculate sum of values of nodes at current level.
   b. Update the max_sum and result_level if the current level sum is greater than max_sum.
4. Return the result_level.

The time complexity of the algorithm is O(n), where n is the number of nodes in the tree since it visits each node once. The space complexity is O(n) as well, as the worst case can have all nodes in the queue at the same time.
